report_name: Instructure Pays Off ShinyHunters to Delete Data of 275M Canvas Users

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A hacking group that conducted a massive data breach against Instructure's Canvas platform, exfiltrating 3.65 TB of data from 275 million users across 9,000 educational institutions and subsequently extorting the company.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through the theft and threatened release of sensitive data.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The attackers exploited a vulnerability in the 'Free-for-Teacher' environment of the Canvas platform to gain their initial foothold.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage Object
attack_patterns.description: The attackers accessed and staged 3.65 TB of data from Canvas's underlying cloud storage infrastructure before exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The 3.65 TB of stolen data was exfiltrated to attacker-controlled infrastructure over their command and control channel.

attack_patterns.name: Data Destruction
attack_patterns.description: The threat of deleting or publicly releasing the stolen data was used as the primary leverage in the extortion against Instructure.

attack_patterns.name: Internal Defacement
attack_patterns.description: The attackers defaced the login portals of affected educational institutions with taunting messages, causing further disruption.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: Assumed to be used for command and control communications and data exfiltration activities.

vulnerabilities.name: Canvas Free-for-Teacher Environment Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: An unspecified vulnerability in the public-facing 'Free-for-Teacher' environment of the Canvas platform was exploited by ShinyHunters for initial access.

identities.name: Instructure
identities.description: The parent company of the Canvas Learning Management System, which was the primary victim of the data breach and entered into an agreement with ShinyHunters.

identities.name: Educational Institutions
identities.description: A class of approximately 9,000 schools and universities globally whose 275 million students and faculty had their data compromised in the Canvas breach.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Exfiltration Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Attacker-controlled infrastructure used to receive the 3.65 TB of data exfiltrated from the Canvas platform.

indicators.name: Anomalous Large Data Egress from Cloud Storage
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Large Data Egress from Cloud Storage
id: 2f8b1c9a-7d3e-4b0f-8c6a-9e1d2f3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects unusually large data egress from cloud storage buckets, which could indicate data exfiltration as seen in the Canvas breach.
  This is an anomaly detection rule and requires baselining.
references:
  - https://www.theguardian.com/technology/article/2026/may/12/canvas-platform-hackers-instructure-deal
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-13
tags:
  - attack.t1530
  - attack.exfiltration
level: medium
logsource:
  category: cloud
  service: s3
detection:
  selection:
    # This is a conceptual rule. Actual fields depend on the log source (e.g., CloudTrail).
    event_name: 'GetObject'
    # Thresholds need to be adjusted based on environment baseline.
    bytes_transferred_out >: 1000000000 # 1 GB
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects large, anomalous data egress traffic from cloud storage buckets (e.g., AWS S3, Azure Blob) associated with platforms like Canvas, which could indicate mass data exfiltration.

indicators.name: Web Shell Deployment on Web Servers
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Web Shell Creation
id: 5a0b3c1d-8e4f-4a1e-9d7b-0c2d3e4f5a6b
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects the creation of files with common web shell extensions in web server directories, a potential sign of compromise.
references:
  - https://www.theguardian.com/technology/article/2026/may/12/canvas-platform-hackers-instructure-deal
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-13
tags:
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.persistence
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '/var/www/'
      - '/srv/www/'
      - '/usr/local/www/apache'
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.php'
      - '.aspx'
      - '.asp'
      - '.jsp'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Hunts for evidence of web shell deployment by monitoring for file creation events with suspicious extensions (e.g., .php, .aspx) in the web directories of application servers like Canvas.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous Large Data Egress from Cloud Storage
Web Shell Deployment on Web Servers
